points.text: Intellia Therapeutics — Lonvoguran (ziclumeran) — Hereditary Angioedema — Scheduled to report topline data from the global Phase 3 HAELO trial, representing the first Phase 3 readout for an in vivo CRISPR gene editing candidate.
Pairwise licensed its Fulcrum® CRISPR platform to Wild Bioscience for AI-driven crop trait development and climate-resilient variety generation.
Research and Markets report projects the Arrayed CRISPR Screening market revenue to grow by $1.64 billion at a 17.7% CAGR through 2030.
Emerging CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing therapies are identified as a key driver igniting hope in the Chronic Granulomatous Disease (CGD) market outlook for 2026-2036.
ATCC and Broad Institute collaborated to engineer 13 CRISPR-edited isogenic non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) cell lines harboring defined resistance mechanisms to the EGFR inhibitor osimertinib.
The CRISPR-engineered NSCLC models will be integrated into the Cancer Dependency Map (DepMap) to construct a response and resistance map (ResMap) for identifying therapeutic vulnerabilities.
A modified CRISPR tool was reported to target the Down syndrome mutation, representing a potential gene-editing approach for the condition.

Erica Larschan (Brown University) — Gene regulation research — X chromosome dosage compensation — Leveraged CRISPR-Cas9 to overcome challenges in editing highly compacted heterochromatic genomic locations previously inaccessible to traditional methods.

Intellia Therapeutics — Lonvoguran (ziclumeran) — Hereditary Angioedema — Phase 3 HAELO trial topline data release scheduled for April 27, 2026; candidate utilizes in vivo CRISPR gene editing technology.

Pairwise — Fulcrum® CRISPR Platform — Agricultural Crop Improvement — Licensing agreement with Wild Bioscience to integrate CRISPR-based genome editing tools into pipeline for row crop yield enhancement and carbon removal projects.
Research and Markets — Arrayed CRISPR Screening — Oncology and Genetic Disease Research — Market revenue projected to increase by $1.64 billion at 17.7% CAGR through 2030.
Research and Markets — CRISPR-Cas9 Gene Editing — Chronic Granulomatous Disease (CGD) — Identified as an emerging therapy alongside lentiviral vectors in the 2026-2036 market analysis.

ATCC / Broad Institute — CRISPR-engineered NSCLC models — Osimertinib resistance — Developed 13 isogenic cell lines using CRISPR gene editing to replicate clinically observed resistance mechanisms for targeted therapy discovery.

Unspecified developers — Modified CRISPR tool — Down syndrome mutation — Tool designed to target specific genetic mutation associated with Down syndrome; mechanism and clinical status not detailed in source.

Brown University (Erica Larschan Lab) — CRISPR-Cas9 application — Fundamental gene regulation — Enabled precise knockout of genes in heterochromatic regions, facilitating 3D genome and single-cell level analysis of X chromosome mechanisms.

Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing commercialization — Lonvoguran (ziclumeran) pipeline — Awarded inducement grants of 67,150 RSUs to 13 new employees in April 2026; advancing first in vivo CRISPR Phase 3 candidate.

Pairwise — CRISPR platform commercialization — Fulcrum® Platform — Co-founded by CRISPR pioneers; licensing proprietary gene editing tools, enzymes, and trait libraries for research and commercial agricultural purposes.
Wild Bioscience — AI-driven trait discovery integration — Fulcrum® CRISPR tools — Partnering with Pairwise to translate computational trait insights into elite crop genetics across multiple species.

ATCC — Precision oncology resource development — CRISPR-engineered isogenic cell lines — Partnered with Broad Institute to release 13 NSCLC models and genomic datasets via DepMap portal for global research access.

Broad Institute — Functional genomics and cancer dependency mapping — CRISPR-engineered resistance models — Co-developed 13 NSCLC lines to accelerate discovery of drug combination strategies and resistance pathways.
Lonvoguran (ziclumeran) — Mechanism: In vivo CRISPR gene editing — Phase: 3 (HAELO) — Population: Hereditary Angioedema patients — Status: Topline data readout scheduled April 27, 2026.

Fulcrum® Platform — Mechanism: CRISPR-based genome editing with proprietary enzymes and trait libraries — Application: Plant trait development — Outcome: Accelerates development of climate-resilient, nutritious crops compared to traditional breeding methods.

CRISPR-Cas9 — Mechanism: Gene editing — Phase: Preclinical/Development (Market Context) — Population: Chronic Granulomatous Disease (CGD) patients — Status: Emerging therapy category in 2026-2036 market outlook.

CRISPR-engineered NSCLC models — Mechanism: CRISPR gene editing and gene overexpression to induce defined resistance — Phase: Preclinical model development — Population: Non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) — Comparator: Drug-sensitive isogenic lines — Endpoints: Replication of osimertinib resistance mechanisms to enable direct comparison of therapeutic vulnerabilities.

Modified CRISPR tool — Mechanism: Gene editing targeting Down syndrome mutation — Phase: Preclinical/early research — Population: Not specified — Comparator: Not specified — Endpoints/Results: Tool identified as targeting mutation; specific efficacy data not provided.

CRISPR-Cas9 — Mechanism: Precise gene editing via RNA-guided nuclease — Phase: Preclinical research tool — Population: Drosophila and mammalian cell models — Comparator: Traditional gene knockout methods — Results: Successfully resolved editing barriers in highly compacted heterochromatic regions, enabling real-time 3D genome observation.

Intellia Therapeutics — Equity incentive expansion — 2024 Inducement Plan — Granted 67,150 time-based RSUs to 13 new employees under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4) to secure talent for CRISPR pipeline advancement.

Pairwise / Wild Bioscience — Licensing agreement — Fulcrum® CRISPR Platform — Granting access to genome editing tools for research and commercial purposes to support yield enhancement and carbon removal projects.
Research and Markets — Arrayed CRISPR Screening Market Report — Market Intelligence — Published April 20, 2026; forecasts $1.64 billion revenue growth at 17.7% CAGR through 2030.
